Title: Machine Learning Scientist
Description:
About Suno

We're building the world's first creative entertainment platform, where the entire world can feel the joy and fulfilment of making music. Music is for everyone: Our users include everyone from grandmothers creating songs for their loved ones, to Grammy winners using Suno Studio, our power tool, to make the most popular hits in the world.

Building the future of entertainment requires ambition. The pace is fast, the problems are hard, and the work demands ownership and intensity. For the right people, it’s incredibly rewarding: a chance to shape a new medium, work with a small team that cares deeply about quality, make music, drink too much coffee, and build something that millions of people use to express themselves in ways that were never before possible.

Suno is the fastest growing consumer entertainment company and the leader in AI music. We are backed by leading investors including Menlo Ventures, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Matrix Partners, NVentures (venture arm of NVIDIA).

About The Role

We’re looking for early members of our research team. You’ll work closely with the founding team and have ownership of a wide variety of technical decisions on how we build and deploy our state of the art ML models trained with an H100/scientist ratio of >100x.

Check out our Suno version of the job here!

What You’ll Need


5+ years experience training state of the art models with distributed pytorch
Intimate familiarity of the entire stack of data engineering, designing, training and evaluating machine learning models
Track record showing independent ownership of entire research projects from start to finish
Extensive experience training large generative models from scratch (LLMs or diffusion models)
A love of music (listening, exploring, making) is a huge plus
Bachelor's degree or equivalent required.


Additional Notes: Applicants must be eligible to work in the US.

Perks & Benefits For Full-Time Employees


Company Equity Package
401(k) with 3% Employer Match & Roth 401(k)
Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance (PPO w/ HSA & FSA options)
11 Paid Holidays + Unlimited PTO & Sick Time
16 Weeks of Paid Parental Leave
Creative Education Stipend
Generous Commuter Allowance
In-Office Lunch (5 days per week)


Suno is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected basis under provincial, federal, state, and local laws, regulations, or ordinances. We will also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of state and local laws, including the Massachusetts Fair Chance in Employment Act, NYC Fair Chance Act, LA City Fair Chance Ordinance, and San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.

Title: Research Intern - Self-Improving AI
Description:
Overview

Research Internships at Microsoft provide a dynamic environment for research careers with a network of world-class research labs led by globally-recognized scientists and engineers, who pursue innovation in a range of scientific and technical disciplines to help solve complex challenges in diverse fields, including computing, healthcare, economics, and the environment.

The Machine Learning/Artificial Intelligence (ML/AI) group at Microsoft Research NYC is looking for a Research Intern candidate with a background in language modeling and reinforcement learning, for a project on self-improving AI systems.

Responsibilities

Research Interns put inquiry and theory into practice. Alongside fellow doctoral candidates and some of the world’s best researchers, Research Interns learn, collaborate, and network for life. Research Interns not only advance their own careers, but they also contribute to exciting research and development strides. During the 12-week internship, Research Interns are paired with mentors and expected to collaborate with other Research Interns and researchers, present findings, and contribute to the vibrant life of the community. Research internships are available in all areas of research, and are offered year-round, though they typically begin in the summer.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications


Enrolled in PhD or master's program in machine learning, computer science, or related discipline.
At least 1 year of experience with deep learning, language modeling, and/or reinforcement learning.


Other Requirements


Research Interns are expected to be physically located in their manager’s Microsoft worksite location for the duration of their internship.
In addition to the qualifications below, you’ll need to submit a minimum of two reference letters for this position as well as a cover letter and any relevant work or research samples. After you submit your application, a request for letters may be sent to your list of references on your behalf. Note that reference letters cannot be requested until after you have submitted your application, and furthermore, that they might not be automatically requested for all candidates. You may wish to alert your letter writers in advance, so they will be ready to submit your letter.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with deep learning and language modeling frameworks including pytorch, huggingface transformers, vLLM.


Applied Sciences IC2 - The base pay range for this internship is USD $5,610 - $11,010 per month.

There is a different range applicable to specific work locations, within the San Francisco Bay area and New York City metropolitan area, and the base pay range for this role in those locations is USD $7,270 - $12,030 per month.

Applied Sciences IC3 - The base pay range for this internship is USD $6,710 - $13,270 per month.

Certain roles may be eligible for benefits and other compensation. Find additional benefits and pay information here: https://careers.microsoft.com/us/en/us-intern-pay

Benefits/perks listed below may vary depending on the nature of your employment with Microsoft and the country where you work.

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

Title: Process Chemist
Description:
Piper Maddox is exclusively partnered with a leading organization in the renewable fuels space seeking a Process Chemist to focus is on developing experimental data and analytical methods to enable scale-up across multiple technologies.




This is a highly hands-on role sitting at the intersection of R&D and commercialization. You’ll work across multiple process pathways, helping translate lab-scale discoveries into scalable, real-world applications. The position offers strong exposure to both bench and pilot environments, with the ability to influence how technologies progress toward commercialization.




Key Responsibilities:

Design and execute experimental programs to support process development, optimization, and scale-up
Develop and refine analytical methods; train and support team members in their application
Generate high-quality data to inform process design, modeling, and decision-making
Work across key unit operations including reactors (batch & continuous), distillation, and fermentation systems
Support both lab-scale and pilot-scale activities, contributing to technology validation and scale progression
Collaborate cross-functionally with engineering and innovation teams to advance multiple parallel projects
Adapt quickly across priorities in a fast-paced, multi-technology environment




Key Requirements:

4+ years of experience in process chemistry, scale-up, or applied R&D environments
Strong foundation in analytical chemistry and method development
Experience in biofuels, renewable chemicals, specialty chemicals, or adjacent industries (pharma/biotech)
Working knowledge of core unit operations (e.g., reaction, separation, fermentation)
Comfortable operating in a dynamic, fast-moving environment with multiple active projects
Hands-on, execution-focused mindset with a bias toward problem-solving and data-driven decision making

Title: Lead Data Scientist
Description:
Job Title: Lead Data Scientist – Revenue Optimization & Pricing

Location: Remote

Compensation: $90–$100 hourly

Job Type: Long Term Contract

Job Summary

We are seeking a Lead Data Scientist to support strategic pricing and revenue optimization initiatives within the onboard revenue organization. This role combines machine learning, operations research, and optimization techniques to drive pricing decisions and maximize revenue outcomes. The ideal candidate will not only build predictive models but also leverage model outputs to develop optimization strategies and prescriptive recommendations that directly influence business decisions. This position requires an individual capable of independently solving complex analytical problems and owning projects from conception through implementation.

Key Responsibilities

Develop machine learning models to support pricing and revenue management initiatives.
Apply optimization and operations research methodologies to business challenges.
Build prescriptive analytics solutions that translate model outputs into actionable recommendations.
Design and implement mathematical optimization frameworks for pricing strategies.
Partner with business stakeholders to solve complex revenue optimization problems.
Work with large-scale datasets using distributed computing technologies.
Lead analytical initiatives from discovery through deployment with minimal oversight.
Deliver measurable business impact through data-driven decision making.

Required Qualifications

7+ years of Data Science experience.
Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience developing and deploying machine learning models.
Experience working with Big Data technologies and large datasets.
Hands-on experience with PySpark.
Proven experience with pricing, revenue optimization, or prescriptive analytics projects.
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to independently lead complex initiatives.
Experience translating analytical findings into business recommendations.

Title: Research Scientist- Reinforcement Learning (Open-Endedness)
Description:
Member Technical Staff- Reinforcement Learning & Open-Ended Learning. Full-time, permanent opportunity in San Francisco.




We're representing an early-stage applied research lab building AI capable of open-ended learning, systems that keep getting better by discovering their own goals rather than optimizing ones we hand them




What you'll do:

Develop RL methods for agents that discover useful objectives, tasks, and curricula without relying entirely on human-specified rewards
Design systems for open-ended learning including unsupervised / automated environment design, asymmetric self-play, and intrinsic motivation
Build training loops where agents learn from interaction, exploration, novelty, competence progress, self-generated challenges, or other nonstandard reward signals
Investigate how agents can avoid collapsing into trivial, degenerate, or easily exploitable objectives
Own and develop a research agenda end to end — from identifying promising directions, to running experiments, to communicating results




What we're looking for

5+ years in reinforcement learning research (PhD and academic years count).
Strong RL fundamentals, with exposure to one or more of: open-endedness, quality-diversity methods, intrinsic motivation, self-play, multi-agent RL, or goal-conditioned RL.
Proficiency in Python and modern ML frameworks (PyTorch and/or JAX)
A track record of research output and/or shipping research-grade code.
High agency and comfort operating in an early-stage, fast-moving research environment.




Nice to have

Experience with LLM post-training and/or coding agents.
Familiarity with population-based training, POET, or related open-ended / evolutionary approaches
Publications at top venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, GECCO, RLC, or similar).

Title: Senior Data Scientist, Product
Description:
About ThredUp

ThredUp is transforming resale with technology and a mission to inspire the world to think secondhand first. By making it easy to buy and sell secondhand, ThredUp has become one of the world's largest online resale platforms for apparel, shoes and accessories. Sellers love ThredUp because we make it easy to clean out their closets and unlock value for themselves or for the charity of their choice while doing good for the planet. Buyers love shopping value, premium and luxury brands all in one place, at up to 90% off estimated retail price. Our proprietary operating platform is the foundation for our managed marketplace and consists of distributed processing infrastructure, proprietary software and systems and data science expertise. With ThredUp’s Resale-as-a-Service, some of the world's leading brands and retailers are leveraging our platform to deliver customizable, scalable resale experiences to their customers. ThredUp has processed over 172 million unique secondhand items from 55,000 brands across 100 categories. By extending the life cycle of clothing, ThredUp is changing the way consumers shop and ushering in a more sustainable future for the fashion industry.




Recognized on TIME Most Influential Companies of 2023, Digiday's WorkLife 50 2023, TIME's Best Inventions of 2022, and Lattice's People Success Awards 2022.




How You Will Make An Impact:

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will combine your technical expertise with your communication skills to own the development of AI and ML models for personalization and product innovation across the marketplace. Reporting to the Director of Product Data Science, you’ll build retrieval & ranking models for product recommendations, leverage multimodal embedding models to create new customer experiences, and identify opportunities through A/B testing & analytics. You’ll own the development of new ML models or major upgrades to existing models, partnering across technical and business teams to deliver experiences that delight our customers.




In This Role You’ll Get To:

Build and Deploy Models: Own end-to-end development of new AI/ML models and major upgrades to existing models used for personalization and the broader marketplace. Put models into production in partnership with engineering partners.
Quantify Impact: Design and analyze experiments (e.g., A/B tests) to measure impact of AI/ML models, optimize the customer experience, and drive business growth.
Enhance Infrastructure: Partner with engineering teams to improve data pipelines, tooling, and infrastructure, enabling efficient experimentation and analysis at scale.
Communicate Cross-functionally: Communicate progress and results to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including senior leadership. Manage stakeholder expectations, effectively guiding conversations on tradeoffs and timelines.
Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Product Managers, Engineers, and Data Scientists to define solutions, educating cross-functionally to elevate the overall technical expertise of the team.
Contribute to Innovation: Stay aware of emerging trends in AI, machine learning, and data science to advance our AI/ML solutions.




What We’re Looking For:

5-7 years in data science, machine learning, or a related field, with a demonstrated track record of owning model upgrades or building new models in a real-world context
MS or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field, or equivalent industry experience
Proficiency in Python and SQL, along with experience in machine learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow) and deploying models at scale
Expertise in deep learning and predictive modeling, including owning evaluation of models via A/B testing. Prior experience implementing recommender systems and working with multimodal embedding models is a plus.
Given a defined project, proven ability to define the path forward and deliver results in a timely manner, thinking ahead to mitigate risks and driving alignment on tradeoffs
Exceptional ability to communicate complex technical concepts clearly and effectively to a variety of audiences. Able to collaborate across technical and business teams to deliver outcomes in a fast-paced environment.
Demonstrated growth mindset. Eager to take on challenges, learn new skills, and mentor others to build a strong team culture.
Background in e-commerce, marketplaces, or a related industry




What We Offer:

Monthly allowance for insurance/education ($200 gross).
50% paid sabbatical after 3 year anniversary.
Paid parental leave for new mothers and fathers.
IT Kit.

Title: Data Analyst I - Revenue Cycle
Description:
At our company, we move fast, adapt quickly, and turn challenges into opportunities—all while keeping quality at the heart of everything we do. We believe that work should be a positive and respectful place, and that with the right mindset, anything is achievable.

Our Vision

Fulfill the essential promise of pharmacy care and help people to live to their healthiest, happiest, and fullest potential.

Our Mission

To be the disruptive force that drives meaningful change within pharmacy services.

Summary

This position is responsible for various billing functions on the Revenue Cycle team. Knowledge of Revenue Cycle within a pharmacy would be extremely helpful in this role. Knowledge of SQL queries and database structure is required to be successful in this role. This position requires the ability to prioritize, plan, and complete tasks in a timely manner.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities


Complete weekly billing functions for certain outsourced services arrangements
Evaluate all billing processes for process improvement opportunities to improve efficiency and accuracy.
Research and provide prompt and courteous follow-up for client related billing questions and/or reporting requests.
Assist in the semi-monthly PBM Billing process, as needed.
Complete the weekly, semi-monthly, and monthly HealthDyne Billing Process, as needed.
Create and distribute other Invoices as needed.
Advanced planning skills; must have the ability to manage tasks to successful timely conclusions.
Assist in developing analytics dashboard to highlight trends in PBM and other functions.
Support the Revenue Cycle team with other duties as assigned.


Education And Experience


Must possess a 4-year degree, preferably in Data Science, Statistics, Healthcare Analytics or related fields, with proven quantitative experience.
1-3 years of work experience
PBM, healthcare, pharmaceutical experience preferred.


Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


High proficiency in Microsoft SQL, R, or other data analysis tools.
Python experience preferred.
Microsoft Excel and Visual Studio experience preferred.
Excellent analytical and critical thinking skills
Intermediate understanding of Tableau or other data visualization tools
Advanced diligence, keeping in mind the “big picture”
Ability to analyze and interpret data based on results.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Ability to listen and understand information and ideas that are presented verbally.


Work Environment / Physical Demands

This position is in both a typical office environment which requires prolonged sitting in front of a computer and in an external environment where travel is required. Requires hand-eye coordination and manual dexterity sufficient to operate standard office equipment including operation of standard computer and phone equipment. May have occasional high stress when dealing with customers/clients. Minimal travel could be required.

EOE M/F/D/V

Title: Vice President of Data & Learning
Description:
THE ROLE

The Vice President (VP) of Data & Learning leads Pahara's data collection, analysis, and organizational learning strategy. This role ensures that data is leveraged effectively to drive decision-making, demonstrate impact, and enable continuous improvement across all programs and operations. The VP of Data & Learning develops systems and practices that build a data-informed culture while also communicating Pahara's impact to external stakeholders.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Area 1: Data Strategy, Analysis, and Insights


Develop a comprehensive data strategy aligned with organizational goals and priorities
Increase the speed and accuracy of actionable data that can be used to achieve Pahara's mission and vision
Define key metrics and indicators that meaningfully reflect Pahara's impact and performance
Identify trends, patterns, and correlations in program and operational data
Lead complex data analysis projects that inform strategic decisions


Area 2: Impact Measurement and Communications


Partner with program leaders to integrate evaluation into program design and delivery
Increase our collective ability to communicate the impact of Pahara's work efficiently and effectively
Partner with communications staff to integrate data into reports, presentations, and other external materials
Support fundraising efforts with relevant data on program outcomes and organizational effectiveness
Lead longitudinal studies that track the influence of Pahara programs over time


Area 3: Data Systems and Infrastructure


Lead the optimization of CRM and other data management platforms and their integration with other organizational systems
Establish data standards, policies, and procedures that ensure data quality and security
Implement data security and privacy protocols that comply with relevant regulations
Develop solutions for data visualization and reporting that make insights accessible


Area 4: Organizational Learning


Foster a culture of inquiry, reflection, and data-informed decision-making
Develop processes for capturing and applying insights across the organization
Facilitate learning sessions that help teams interpret and act on data insights
Create feedback loops that connect data collection to organizational improvement
Document and share organizational learning through appropriate knowledge management systems


Area 5: Collaboration and Team Leadership


Partner with program team to ensure data collection and evaluation meet program needs
Collaborate with operations teams to implement efficient data collection processes
Manage the Data Manager and build team capacity through training and development
Establish clear team goals, roles, and performance expectations
Identify and address skill gaps through hiring, professional development, or external partnerships
Complete other duties at the discretion of your manager


Supervisory Responsibilities


Direct supervision of the Data Manager
Functional leadership for data initiatives across the organization
Collaboration with and guidance to staff on data collection and analysis


Additional Details


Reporting Relationships: The VP of Data & Learning reports to the Executive Vice President of Growth & Operations.
Work Environment: We are a virtual organization by design so that our team can work where they have built their lives. Lone Rock, our campus in the Denver area, serves as a hub for our work and as the main location for all in-person events.
Travel: This position will require significant travel to Lone Rock Campus for various purposes, including organizational meetings and events, operations support during programming, and other job-specific purposes. When at Lone Rock, you must be able to remain in a stationary position 50% of the time at a higher altitude while on campus and occasionally move boxes weighing up to 30 pounds for various needs. Seminar days often begin early and end late, so you should be able to commit to an unconventional schedule when participating in programming, as opposed to a 9am - 5pm schedule.
Compensation: Pahara provides competitive compensation and an attractive benefits package, including full health care coverage and access to a 401(k) account with a matching contribution policy. The salary for this role is within the salary band for Vice Presidents at Pahara.


Requirements

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES


Master's degree in data science, evaluation, research methods, or related field preferred
8+ years of experience in data management, program evaluation, or related fields
Experience in education, nonprofit, or social impact assessment preferred
Strong understanding of quantitative and qualitative research methods
Expertise in data analysis, visualization, and interpretation
Knowledge of data management platforms and business intelligence tools
Experience designing and implementing program evaluation frameworks
Strong project management and organizational skills
Superior written and verbal communication abilities
Ability to translate complex data into accessible insights for diverse audiences
Strategic thinking and problem-solving capabilities
Knowledge of data privacy regulations and ethical data practices
Ability to travel occasionally for organizational meetings and events
Proficient with data analysis tools, CRM systems, and relevant technology platforms
Alignment with Pahara's core values


MUST-HAVES FOR VICE PRESIDENT-LEVEL ROLES


The VP's primary focus is to lead data strategy and organizational learning for Pahara.
The VP develops, implements, monitors, and refines data systems and learning practices with high levels of autonomy. They translate organizational needs into effective approaches for gathering, analyzing, and applying data to drive impact and improvement.
The VP facilitates collaboration across the organization to ensure data quality and usefulness, bringing analytical expertise and evaluation best practices to all aspects of Pahara's work.
You are a strategic data leader with exceptional analytical abilities. You have demonstrated success in designing evaluation approaches and building data-informed cultures. You are intellectually curious, methodologically rigorous, and committed to continuous learning. You excel at both technical data work and organizational change management.
You are committed to leveraging data to advance educational transformation. You understand how thoughtful evaluation contributes to organizational effectiveness and program impact. You recognize the connection between data-informed decision-making and achieving educational equity. You believe in Pahara's mission to develop diverse, high-integrity leaders who can redefine public education.
You excel at making data meaningful and accessible to diverse stakeholders. You communicate effectively across technical and non-technical audiences. You approach data work with integrity and an equity lens. You model Pahara's values through your work and contribute to an organizational culture of learning and improvement.

  • Identify and address skill gaps through hiring, professional development, or external partnerships
  • Complete other duties at the discretion of your manager

Supervisory Responsibilities

  • Direct supervision of the Data Manager
  • Functional leadership for data initiatives across the organization
  • Collaboration with and guidance to staff on data collection and analysis

Additional Details

  • Reporting Relationships: The VP of Data & Learning reports to the Executive Vice President of Growth & Operations.
  • Work Environment: We are a virtual organization by design so that our team can work where they have built their lives. Lone Rock, our campus in the Denver area, serves as a hub for our work and as the main location for all in-person events.
  • Travel: This position will require significant travel to Lone Rock Campus for various purposes, including organizational meetings and events, operations support during programming, and other job-specific purposes. When at Lone Rock, you must be able to remain in a stationary position 50% of the time at a higher altitude while on campus and occasionally move boxes weighing up to 30 pounds for various needs. Seminar days often begin early and end late, so you should be able to commit to an unconventional schedule when participating in programming, as opposed to a 9am - 5pm schedule.
  • Compensation: Pahara provides competitive compensation and an attractive benefits package, including full health care coverage and access to a 401(k) account with a matching contribution policy. The salary for this role is within the salary band for Vice Presidents at Pahara.

Requirements

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES

  • Master's degree in data science, evaluation, research methods, or related field preferred
  • 8+ years of experience in data management, program evaluation, or related fields
  • Experience in education, nonprofit, or social impact assessment preferred
  • Strong understanding of quantitative and qualitative research methods
  • Expertise in data analysis, visualization, and interpretation
  • Knowledge of data management platforms and business intelligence tools
  • Experience designing and implementing program evaluation frameworks
  • Strong project management and organizational skills
  • Superior written and verbal communication abilities
  • Ability to translate complex data into accessible insights for diverse audiences
  • Strategic thinking and problem-solving capabilities
  • Knowledge of data privacy regulations and ethical data practices
  • Ability to travel occasionally for organizational meetings and events
  • Proficient with data analysis tools, CRM systems, and relevant technology platforms
  • Alignment with Pahara's core values

MUST-HAVES FOR VICE PRESIDENT-LEVEL ROLES

  • The VP's primary focus is to lead data strategy and organizational learning for Pahara.
  • The VP develops, implements, monitors, and refines data systems and learning practices with high levels of autonomy. They translate organizational needs into effective approaches for gathering, analyzing, and applying data to drive impact and improvement.
  • The VP facilitates collaboration across the organization to ensure data quality and usefulness, bringing analytical expertise and evaluation best practices to all aspects of Pahara's work.
  • You are a strategic data leader with exceptional analytical abilities. You have demonstrated success in designing evaluation approaches and building data-informed cultures. You are intellectually curious, methodologically rigorous, and committed to continuous learning. You excel at both technical data work and organizational change management.
  • You are committed to leveraging data to advance educational transformation. You understand how thoughtful evaluation contributes to organizational effectiveness and program impact. You recognize the connection between data-informed decision-making and achieving educational equity. You believe in Pahara's mission to develop diverse, high-integrity leaders who can redefine public education.
  • You excel at making data meaningful and accessible to diverse stakeholders. You communicate effectively across technical and non-technical audiences. You approach data work with integrity and an equity lens. You model Pahara's values through your work and contribute to an organizational culture of learning and improvement.

Seeking a Data Scientist to assist with designing, development, and deployment of advanced analytics and machine learning solutions. Job Responsibilities Of The Data Scientist


Design, build, validate, and deploy machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions in production environments.
Analyze large, complex structured and unstructured datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities.

Leverage cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and modern data stack technologies to improve model performance and data workflows.

Proficiency in Python (e.g., pandas, scikit-learn, NumPy) and SQL
Experience building, validating, and deploying machine learning models

Experience working with large, complex datasets (structured and unstructured)
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex concepts into business‑friendly insights
Collaborative mindset with experience influencing stakeholders across technical and non‑technical teams

Bonus Experience Of The Data Scientist


Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and modern data stacks

Title: Quantum Research Scientist with Python - Freelance AI Trainer
HTML:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:

  • Design original computational physics problems that simulate real physics research workflows;
  • Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Numpy, SciPy, Sympy);
  • Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks);
  • Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in mechanics, electromagnetism, thermodynamics, and quantum mechanics;
  • Base problems on real research challenges or practical applications from physics practice;
  • Verify solutions using Python with standard physics simulation libraries;
  • Document problem statements clearly and provide verified correct answers.

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for quantum researchers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • Degree in Physics (Theoretical, Experimental, or Computational) or related fields;
  • Python proficiency for numerical validation. MATLAB, R, C, SQL, Numpy, Pandas, SciPy, domain-specific libraries, Stata or knowledge of any programming language can be equivalent;
  • 2+ years of professional experience: applied, research, or teaching experience is applicable;
  • Experience with numerical simulation methods;
  • Ability to design problems that mirror real physics research workflows;
  • Creative thinking in problem design across diverse physics areas;
  • Familiarity with physics modeling and approximation techniques;
  • Strong written English (C1+).

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $76 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Process Development Scientist St. Louis
Description:
Work Location: St. Louis, Missouri

Shift: No

Department: LS-PS-ATA PDS Region 1

Hiring Manager: Ben Roman

This information is for internals only. Please do not share outside of the organization.

Your Role

MilliporeSigma is looking for a Process Development Scientist to join the technical branch of the commercial organization in the Midwest, working remotely from Indianapolis, Chicago, St. Louis or Minneapolis, and supporting process development and manufacturing sciences activities. The development scientist is part of a worldwide team of engineers and scientists reporting into the technical support group for MilliporeSigma's Process Solutions business.


Lead development studies for purification of therapeutic proteins using MilliporeSigma technologies (sterile filtration, depth filtration, tangential flow filtration, viral reduction filtration, chromatography)
Involve in selection/sizing, process optimization, and tech transfer for purification of new pharmaceuticals, focused on Phase I-III development
Provide customer consultations via email, telephone, or video
Collaborate with customer process development groups in the Central United States
Partner with local MilliporeSigma account managers to deliver technical expertise to customers
Support clinical and commercial scale implementations using sound engineering principles, including identifying CQAs and maintaining critical control parameters
Document customer interfacing activities in electronic systems; propose new technical ideas for the broader group and contribute to cross-functional strategy execution
This position will include travel of up to 30% within North America region


Physical Attributes


Lifting of up to 50 pounds


Minimum Qualifications

Who you are:


Bachelor's degree in Chemical Engineering, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Sciences, or other Life Science discipline
3+ years of experience within pharmaceutical development, technology implementation, or protein or viral particle purification


-OR-


Master's degree in Chemical Engineering, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Sciences, or other Life Science discipline
2+ years of experience within pharmaceutical development, technology implementation, or protein or viral particle purification


-OR-


PhD in Chemical Engineering, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Sciences, or other Life Sciences discipline
1+ years of experience within pharmaceutical development, technology implementation, or protein or viral particle purification


Preferred Qualifications


An in-depth knowledge and understanding of bioprocess unit operations
Ability to derive relevant conclusions, based on the experimental results, and effectively communicate this to our customers
Excellent hands on laboratory skills, working knowledge of Microsoft Excel or other data analysis software
Sound understanding of downstream and/or upstream biomanufacturing process requirements, best practices, and industry standards
Comfort with making recommendations for future course of action based on experimental results
Ability to relay relevant results to sales, field marketing, etc.
Ability to identify scientific leadership opportunities and execute through lunch & learns, publications, presentations, etc. with a focus on customer collaboration
Ability to cope with change, adjust test plans quickly, and comfortably handle risk and uncertainty
Ability to build effective relationships; both internally and externally
Ability to produce quality written experimental reports and other appropriate documentation
Highly motivated, self-directed, and able to work independently in a field-based position
Demonstrated ability to communicate clearly, concisely and effectively to diverse audiences through both written and oral communications


Pay Range for this position: $89,800-$134,700.

Title: Founding AI & Backend Engineer
Description:
Nightwave AI is a law enforcement AI startup building the next generation of technology that help detectives solve crimes more effectively, efficiently, and accurately.




The goal is simple: give investigators the kind of intelligence capability that only the largest agencies in the world can afford today, and put it in the hands of every department. We are a small, fast-moving team working on a problem that genuinely matters, and we are looking to connect with talented, technically driven builders who want to do serious work on a serious product.




We are committed to advancing modern investigative methods while ensuring ethical and efficient processes.




Role Description

Nightwave's product surface is where AI becomes real and useful to detectives. But the engine, harness, architecture, and overall structure underneath is where the most difficult and messy problems live. This role owns that engine.




You will be one of the first founding engineers at Nightwave, working directly with the founder and founding team. Your primary focus is the AI, backend, architecture, and systems core: the agent harness that powers our frontier SOTA investigative reasoning, the data pipelines that ingest messy real-world evidence and external sources, the ontology that structures everything we know about a case and eve multiple cases across multiple agencies, and the architecture and tech stackthat holds it all together. Your secondary focus is helping the team move fast across the stack when the situation calls for it, collaborating with UI/UX developers and design work, sales and marketing for pitches and deliverables, and directly with the founder for high level strategic decision-making, both technical and non-technical.




For this to actually work in the real world, the underlying systems have to be fast, reliable, secure, and smart. That is what you will be building.

You will own how Nightwave actually thinks and behaves, working on our core competitive advantage, helping bring justice to more criminals, and having complete power and control over the entire architecture and system design. That is a huge responsibility and we need someone who understands it, and can meet the heavy requirements.




What You'll Actually Be Doing

Designing and building the core agent harness: agent harness, subagents, tools, Skills, memory, planning, evals, monitoring loops, context management, feedback loops, and guardrails for investigative agents that operate on real case data
Building and maintaining the data pipelines that ingest, normalize, and structure evidence from dozens of both structured, unstructured, and external sources — PDFs, documents, media files, exports from forensic tools, agency record systems and existing evidence management systems, and public data + open source intelligence
Architecting the Nightwave ontology; the semantic backbone that lets agents reason about people, places, events, evidence, leads, theories, and relationships within and across cases in complex, sophisticated ways based on the task at hand
Designing backend systems, microservices, functions and tools, modules, and API layers that the frontend and agents both depend on (primarily Python with FastAPI, with flexibility where it makes sense)
Integrating with external data sources and open source intelligence tooling; figuring out what's useful, what's reliable, and how to pull signal out of a lot of noise
Building the evaluation and observability infrastructure that tells us whether the agent is actually working, where it fails, and how to make it better; being able to create the best update, compare to the old version through rigorous evals and realistic manual use on real difficult criminal cases, then actually update, then iterate and repeat
Making architecture decisions that will define how Nightwave scales to hundreds of agencies ; performance, speed, reliability, cost, and security all matter
Working inside a CJIS-aware environment and helping shape how we handle sensitive law enforcement data responsibly and compliantly; must be a US citizen to handle this sensitive data and work at Nightwave
Shipping. Constantly. We need someone who builds, learns, and rebuilds; not someone who designs for six weeks before writing code

Who You Are

A strong systems thinker with deep backend fundamentals. You understand distributed systems, data modeling, simple and clear code, API design, and the tradeoffs that matter when you're handling real, messy, high-stakes data
Experienced with modern AI and agent systems; LLM orchestration, tool use, Skills, memory, context management, subagents, continuous learning and improvement, post-training with RL and fine-tuning, retrieval, evaluation loops, guardrails. You have opinions and know about what works and what doesn't in production
Fluent in Python and comfortable in the FastAPI + async ecosystem. Bonus fluency in whatever else is useful: Go, Rust, TypeScript, SQL, infrastructure-as-code (React for frontend)
Someone who has built with ontologies, graph databases, RAG architectures, complex data pipelines, and knowledge graphs; or who has the depth to learn them fast (most important attribute we are looking for is speed of learning)
Experienced with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and the practical tradeoffs of running real systems; cost, latency, reliability, observability, security
Security-minded. You think about what could go wrong with sensitive data before anyone has to ask; CJIS-compliance and top-tier security standards are monumental for Nightwave and any product working in this industry
Entrepreneurial and self-directed. At this stage, the person building it is the person deciding how it should work. You need to be comfortable making architecture calls and owning them
Someone who has shipped real systems and knows the difference between a demo that works once and software that holds up under daily use by real operators
Preferably based in or willing to be in the Columbus, Ohio area (hybrid, with real in-person time)

Bonus If You Have

Experience building agent frameworks or harnesses from scratch — or deep work with existing ones like LangGraph, Deep Agents SDK or Claude Agent SDK, or custom orchestration layers
Background in high-stakes operational environments: law enforcement, defense, intelligence, fraud, healthcare, or anywhere correctness and security are non-negotiable
Experience with CJIS, FedRAMP, SOC 2, or other compliance frameworks
Familiarity with OSINT tooling, forensic data formats, or investigative workflows
Knowledge graph or ontology design experience; Palantir Foundry or Gotham, Neo4j, RDF, or custom graph architectures
Experience with large-scale unstructured (and structured) data pipelines — audio, video, documents, structured exports
Time at a similar mission-driven startup, Palantir, Anduril, Flock Safety, or similar

What We Offer

Competitive salary plus meaningful early-stage equity, with flexibility on how we structure the package between cash and equity depending on what matters most to you
Direct work with law enforcement; the systems you build will power real investigations and solve real crimes
A founder who is technical and will be building alongside you
The chance to define how crimes get solved in the future and see them get solved; bringing justice to vicious criminals

Nightwave AI is a law enforcement AI startup building the next generation of technology that help detectives solve crimes more effectively, efficiently, and accurately.


The goal is simple: give investigators the kind of intelligence capability that only the largest agencies in the world can afford today, and put it in the hands of every department. We are a small, fast-moving team working on a problem that genuinely matters, and we are looking to connect with talented, technically driven builders who want to do serious work on a serious product.


We are committed to advancing modern investigative methods while ensuring ethical and efficient processes.


Role Description

Nightwave's product surface is where AI becomes real and useful to detectives. But the engine, harness, architecture, and overall structure underneath is where the most difficult and messy problems live. This role owns that engine.


You will be one of the first founding engineers at Nightwave, working directly with the founder and founding team. Your primary focus is the AI, backend, architecture, and systems core: the agent harness that powers our frontier SOTA investigative reasoning, the data pipelines that ingest messy real-world evidence and external sources, the ontology that structures everything we know about a case and eve multiple cases across multiple agencies, and the architecture and tech stackthat holds it all together. Your secondary focus is helping the team move fast across the stack when the situation calls for it, collaborating with UI/UX developers and design work, sales and marketing for pitches and deliverables, and directly with the founder for high level strategic decision-making, both technical and non-technical.


For this to actually work in the real world, the underlying systems have to be fast, reliable, secure, and smart. That is what you will be building.

You will own how Nightwave actually thinks and behaves, working on our core competitive advantage, helping bring justice to more criminals, and having complete power and control over the entire architecture and system design. That is a huge responsibility and we need someone who understands it, and can meet the heavy requirements.


What You'll Actually Be Doing

  • Designing and building the core agent harness: agent harness, subagents, tools, Skills, memory, planning, evals, monitoring loops, context management, feedback loops, and guardrails for investigative agents that operate on real case data
  • Building and maintaining the data pipelines that ingest, normalize, and structure evidence from dozens of both structured, unstructured, and external sources — PDFs, documents, media files, exports from forensic tools, agency record systems and existing evidence management systems, and public data + open source intelligence
  • Architecting the Nightwave ontology; the semantic backbone that lets agents reason about people, places, events, evidence, leads, theories, and relationships within and across cases in complex, sophisticated ways based on the task at hand
  • Designing backend systems, microservices, functions and tools, modules, and API layers that the frontend and agents both depend on (primarily Python with FastAPI, with flexibility where it makes sense)
  • Integrating with external data sources and open source intelligence tooling; figuring out what's useful, what's reliable, and how to pull signal out of a lot of noise
  • Building the evaluation and observability infrastructure that tells us whether the agent is actually working, where it fails, and how to make it better; being able to create the best update, compare to the old version through rigorous evals and realistic manual use on real difficult criminal cases, then actually update, then iterate and repeat
  • Making architecture decisions that will define how Nightwave scales to hundreds of agencies ; performance, speed, reliability, cost, and security all matter
  • Working inside a CJIS-aware environment and helping shape how we handle sensitive law enforcement data responsibly and compliantly; must be a US citizen to handle this sensitive data and work at Nightwave
  • Shipping. Constantly. We need someone who builds, learns, and rebuilds; not someone who designs for six weeks before writing code

Who You Are

  • A strong systems thinker with deep backend fundamentals. You understand distributed systems, data modeling, simple and clear code, API design, and the tradeoffs that matter when you're handling real, messy, high-stakes data
  • Experienced with modern AI and agent systems; LLM orchestration, tool use, Skills, memory, context management, subagents, continuous learning and improvement, post-training with RL and fine-tuning, retrieval, evaluation loops, guardrails. You have opinions and know about what works and what doesn't in production
  • Fluent in Python and comfortable in the FastAPI + async ecosystem. Bonus fluency in whatever else is useful: Go, Rust, TypeScript, SQL, infrastructure-as-code (React for frontend)
  • Someone who has built with ontologies, graph databases, RAG architectures, complex data pipelines, and knowledge graphs; or who has the depth to learn them fast (most important attribute we are looking for is speed of learning)
  • Experienced with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and the practical tradeoffs of running real systems; cost, latency, reliability, observability, security
  • Security-minded. You think about what could go wrong with sensitive data before anyone has to ask; CJIS-compliance and top-tier security standards are monumental for Nightwave and any product working in this industry
  • Entrepreneurial and self-directed. At this stage, the person building it is the person deciding how it should work. You need to be comfortable making architecture calls and owning them
  • Someone who has shipped real systems and knows the difference between a demo that works once and software that holds up under daily use by real operators
  • Preferably based in or willing to be in the Columbus, Ohio area (hybrid, with real in-person time)

Bonus If You Have

  • Experience building agent frameworks or harnesses from scratch — or deep work with existing ones like LangGraph, Deep Agents SDK or Claude Agent SDK, or custom orchestration layers
  • Background in high-stakes operational environments: law enforcement, defense, intelligence, fraud, healthcare, or anywhere correctness and security are non-negotiable
  • Experience with CJIS, FedRAMP, SOC 2, or other compliance frameworks
  • Familiarity with OSINT tooling, forensic data formats, or investigative workflows
  • Knowledge graph or ontology design experience; Palantir Foundry or Gotham, Neo4j, RDF, or custom graph architectures
  • Experience with large-scale unstructured (and structured) data pipelines — audio, video, documents, structured exports
  • Time at a similar mission-driven startup, Palantir, Anduril, Flock Safety, or similar

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary plus meaningful early-stage equity, with flexibility on how we structure the package between cash and equity depending on what matters most to you
  • Direct work with law enforcement; the systems you build will power real investigations and solve real crimes
  • A founder who is technical and will be building alongside you
  • The chance to define how crimes get solved in the future and see them get solved; bringing justice to vicious criminals

Title: Senior Data Scientist (Remote)
Description:
Role: Senior Data Scientist (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $59 - $114/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Senior Data Scientist to work on a contractor basis. This person will play a crucial role in designing, developing, and implementing advanced machine learning models for ai training initiatives, analyzing and interpreting large, complex datasets to guide project strategies and decision-making. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work with a global leader in the technology industry, applying their expertise to help train next-generation ai systems and shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.




Key Responsibilities:

• Design, develop, and implement advanced machine learning models for ai training initiatives, utilizing expertise in machine learning, statistical analysis, and data mining.

• Analyze and interpret large, complex datasets to guide project strategies and decision-making, identifying trends, patterns, and insights that inform business decisions.

• Develop and maintain technical documentation, ensuring that all projects are well-documented and easily accessible to stakeholders.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams, providing expertise and guidance on data analysis, model development, and performance testing.

• Ensure data privacy and security, implementing measures to protect sensitive information and maintain compliance with industry regulations.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and R, with experience in machine learning, statistical analysis, and data mining.

• Strong understanding of deep learning, natural language processing, and reinforcement learning, with the ability to apply these concepts to real-world problems.

• Experience with cloud computing platforms such as AWS, GCP, and Azure, with the ability to design and implement large-scale data processing systems.

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work autonomously and collaboratively as part of a remote team.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the technology industry, applying expertise to help train next-generation ai systems and shape the future of artificial intelligence. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work on complex, high-impact projects, collaborating with a team of experts to drive innovation and growth.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

Title: Part-Time Research Assistant, PCAT Project
Description:
APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS:


CURRENT PENN STATE EMPLOYEE (faculty, staff, technical service, or student), please login to Workday to complete the internal application process. Please do not apply here, apply internally through Workday.
CURRENT PENN STATE STUDENT (not employed previously at the university) and seeking employment with Penn State, please login to Workday to complete the student application process. Please do not apply here, apply internally through Workday.
If you are NOT a current employee or student, please click “Apply” and complete the application process for external applicants.


Approval of remote and hybrid work is not guaranteed regardless of work location. For additional information on remote work at Penn State, see Notice to Out of State Applicants.

Job Description And Position Requirements

The PCAT Lab is recruiting 2 undergraduate research assistants for Summer 2026.

Research assistant will help with in-person data collection and behavioral coding. Data collection tasks include conducting Zoom interviews with parents, setting up the testing space, equipment setup (e.g., fNIRS, EEG, ECG, mobile eye tracking), and interacting with families. Behavioral coding tasks include coding parent-child interaction tasks, completing CITI Training, and utilizing Google Calendar, Slack, Canvas, and Microsoft OneDrive. Research assistants will receive training on data collection tasks, behavioral coding tasks, and handling equipment.

BACKGROUND CHECKS/CLEARANCES

This position requires the following clearances in addition to applicable background checks: PA State Police Criminal Background Check, PA Child Abuse History Clearance Form, and Federal (FBI) Fingerprint Criminal Background Check.

CAMPUS SECURITY CRIME STATISTICS

Pursuant to the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act and the Pennsylvania Act of 1988, Penn State publishes a combined Annual Security and Annual Fire Safety Report (ASR). The ASR includes crime statistics and institutional policies concerning campus security, such as those concerning alcohol and drug use, crime prevention, the reporting of crimes, sexual assault, and other matters. The ASR is available for review here.

EEO IS THE LAW

Penn State is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing employment opportunities to all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If you are unable to use our online application process due to an impairment or disability, please contact 814-865-1473.

Penn State is committed to and accountable for advancing equity, respect, and belonging. We embrace individual uniqueness, as well as a culture of belonging that supports equity initiatives, leverages the educational and institutional benefits of inclusion in society, and provides opportunities for engagement intended to help all members of the community thrive. We value belonging as a core strength and an essential element of the university’s teaching, research, and service mission.
